message:
My friend Sarah texts the group chat at least once a month about some hypothetical disaster scenario—usually at 11 PM, usually all caps. Last week it was a power outage, the week before that a water main break. Honestly, I used to roll my eyes.

Then my actual basement flooded (thanks, spring). And you know who had already thought through where important documents should live, had a checklist of what to grab, and didn't panic? Sarah.

Turns out there's a difference between doom-scrolling and actual readiness. Being prepared doesn't mean you're living in fear—it just means when life gets messy (literally, in my case), you're not also scrambling to figure out the basics. A little thought ahead of time? Chef's kiss.

Have you got a friend like Sarah, or are you the one always thinking three steps ahead? 😄
